name: treehouse
description: Create, reopen, and remove Git worktrees from VS Code across local and SSH-backed targets.

commands.title: Treehouse: Open App Preview in Browser
Treehouse: Open App Preview in VS Code

treehouse.alwaysPromptForTarget.description: Whether Treehouse should prompt for the target before showing repositories instead of using the unified picker.

treehouse.defaultCreateTarget.description: Which target Treehouse should use by default for new clones when the repository picker itself does not already imply a target.

treehouse.targets.description: Configured Treehouse targets. Local is always available, and SSH targets use your existing ssh host aliases.

treehouse.repositoriesRoot.markdownDeprecationMessage: Deprecated. Treehouse now reads local repository roots from `treehouse.targets`.
treehouse.repositoriesRoot.description: Deprecated. Directory containing the primary Git repositories.

treehouse.worktreesRoot.markdownDeprecationMessage: Deprecated. Treehouse now reads local worktree roots from `treehouse.targets`.
treehouse.worktreesRoot.description: Deprecated. Directory under which managed worktrees are created.

treehouse.worktreePathTemplate.description: Relative folder template under the worktrees root. Supported placeholders: ${repo}, ${branch}, ${sanitizedBranch}.

treehouse.envFileMode.markdownDescription: How `.env` should be added to new worktrees: `link`, `copy`, or `off`.

treehouse.installDependencies.description: Whether `pnpm i` should run automatically for newly created worktrees.

enumDescriptions: Open app previews in the system browser.
Open app previews in VS Code's integrated browser.
treehouse.appPreviewOpenTarget.description: Where Treehouse should open app preview URLs.

treehouse.codingAgentCommand.description: Shell command prefix used to launch the coding agent. Treehouse appends the implementation prompt as the final argument. Example: `codex` or `claude code`. When a path-like executable is missing on the execution host, Treehouse falls back to its basename on PATH.

treehouse.codexSessionsRoot.description: Root folder containing Codex session history files. `~` is expanded on the current workspace host.

treehouse.codexSessionPollIntervalMs.description: Polling interval in milliseconds for watching Codex session JSONL files. Treehouse uses polling so this works on SSH hosts and Linux filesystems.

treehouse.openLatestCodexChatOnStartup.description: Whether Treehouse should open the latest Codex chat for the current workspace when the VS Code window starts or reloads.

enumDescriptions: Open the conversation through the OpenAI Codex extension sidebar route.
Open the conversation as an OpenAI Codex custom editor tab.
treehouse.latestCodexChatOpenTarget.description: Where Treehouse should open the latest Codex chat.

treehouse.commitMessageCodexCommand.description: Shell command prefix Treehouse should use to run Codex for commit title generation. Treehouse appends `exec`, read-only automation flags, and the rendered prompt. Example: `codex` or `codex --profile work`. When a path-like executable is missing on the execution host, Treehouse falls back to its basename on PATH.

treehouse.commitMessagePrompt.markdownDescription: Prompt template used to generate commit titles for `Treehouse: Commit and Push`. Available placeholders: `${repoPath}`, `${branchName}`, `${branchIssueId}`, `${targetLabel}`, `${gitStatus}`, `${gitDiffStat}`, `${gitDiff}`, `${gitCachedDiffStat}`, `${gitCachedDiff}`, and `${untrackedDiffs}`.

treehouse.commitMessageRequireApproval.description: Whether `Treehouse: Commit and Push` should pause for manual review of the generated commit title before committing.

treehouse.commitAndPushPullRequestMode.markdownDescription: Pull request mode used by `Treehouse: Commit and Push` on the first push. `ready` creates a normal PR and `draft` creates a draft PR.

treehouse.commitAndPushRunCheckoutPullRe.description: Whether `Treehouse: Commit and Push` should silently run `gh pr checkout` after successfully creating a new pull request on the branch's first push, so the GitHub Pull Requests extension can detect the active PR without showing its checkout picker.

treehouse.skillsRoot.description: Folder containing available agent skills. Relative paths are resolved from the current workspace when possible.

treehouse.executeSkillsInBackground.description: Whether `Treehouse: Execute Agent Skill` should run the selected skill in the background with a notification instead of opening a terminal editor.

treehouse.shellCommand.description: Optional shell executable Treehouse should use for local commands instead of the current SHELL environment variable.

treehouse.linearApiKey.markdownDescription: Linear personal API key used to fetch issue details for `Treehouse: Implement Linear Issue`. Stored in local VS Code settings as plain text.

treehouse.linearAssignedIssueFilters.markdownDescription: Additional filters applied to your assigned Linear issues in the picker and sidebar. Entries are combined with `AND`. Supported fields: `label:"Today"`, `state:"In Progress"`, `team:"AI"`, `project:"Roadmap"`, `id:"AI-123"`, `title:"preview"`, or free-text search.

treehouse.linearAssignedIssuesGroupBy.markdownDescription: How the Treehouse Linear sidebar groups your assigned issues.

treehouse.linearTeamRepositoryMap.markdownDescription: Map Linear team keys or names to repository folder names. Example: `{ "SNACKS": "waffle-maker" }`.

treehouse.linearSetIssueInProgress.description: Whether `Treehouse: Implement Linear Issue` should move the selected Linear issue into an in-progress state before opening its worktree.

treehouse.linearStartCodingAgent.description: Whether `Treehouse: Implement Linear Issue` should automatically open a terminal editor tab and start the configured coding agent after opening the worktree.

treehouse.openInNewWindow.description: Whether Treehouse should prefer opening worktrees in a new VS Code window when the current window is empty. Treehouse always uses a new window when the current window already has a folder open.
